Title: Molecular insights into bacteroid development during Rhizobium–legume symbiosis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant nitrogen nutrition and growth via symbiotic nitrogen fixation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for bacteroid differentiation and survival in NCR-producing legumes, thereby enabling effective nitrogen fixation and ammonia supply to the host.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BacA is essential for successful nitrogen-fixing symbiosis in NCR-producing IRLC legumes because bacA mutants are killed after release into host cells instead of differentiating into bacteroids. 2. BacA protects rhizobia from antimicrobial NCR peptides, including NCR247-induced membrane permeabilization. 3. BacA also affects lipid A very-long-chain fatty acid content, but peptide-protection and VLCFA-related functions can be genetically uncoupled.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABC transporter-related inner membrane protein; proposed transmembrane domain of an ABC transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis in symbiosis; complementation with Brucella abortus bacA and Escherichia coli sbmA; peptide uptake assays; in vitro NCR247 viability assays; lipid A analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article. BacA requirement correlates with legumes producing NCR peptides; dispensable in several phaseoloid legumes lacking NCR peptides. BacA homologs in Brucella abortus and Mycobacterium tuberculosis are discussed as functionally related in chronic intracellular persistence.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: bacA loss abolishes effective nitrogen-fixing symbiosis in NCR-producing hosts; bacterial death in nodules
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering more robust rhizobial inoculants compatible with NCR-producing legumes; should be combined with cell-envelope protective traits.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NCR peptide challenge in symbiosomes; in vitro treatment with folded/reduced NCR247; intracellular nodule environment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/genome resources for legumes discussed generally; no gene-specific accession provided

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports plant nitrogen acquisition through efficient bacteroid-mediated nitrogen fixation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drive terminal bacteroid differentiation and influence bacteroid survival, indirectly promoting effective nitrogen fixation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCR genes encode nodule-specific cysteine-rich peptides specifically expressed in infected nodule cells of IRLC legumes. 2. NCR peptides traffic to symbiosomes and enter bacteroids, where they induce bacteroid-like differentiation features such as cell elongation and increased DNA content. 3. Some NCR peptides are antimicrobial and can permeabilize bacterial membranes, making them both differentiation and survival determinants.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nodule-specific cysteine-rich peptide family; defensin-like cysteine-rich peptides with N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secretory pathway; symbiosome compartment; within bacteroids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression profiling; promoter-GUS/in situ hybridization; immunolocalization; fluorescent peptide fusions; heterologous expression in Lotus japonicus; in vitro peptide treatment of S. meliloti
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article. Nearly 600 NCR genes identified in Medicago truncatula, with >300 confirmed expressed. Distribution largely restricted to IRLC legumes. Different NCRs show distinct spatiotemporal expression and not all have identical antimicrobial activity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong antimicrobial activity can kill rhizobia lacking sufficient protection such as BacA; complexity of large peptide family complicates engineering
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Nodule-specific; subsets expressed in young infected cells or mature symbiotic cells
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tools to modulate bacteroid differentiation and symbiotic efficiency; selected peptides such as NCR247 may be exploitable as antimicrobials.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nodule developmental stage; symbiosome targeting; in vitro peptide treatment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Legume genome sequences, EST clustering, microarray transcriptome analyses mentioned; no accession provided

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrogen-fixing symbiosis by permitting NCR peptide delivery and bacteroid differentiation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for trafficking of NCR peptides to symbiosomes; dnf1 mutants fail to support bacteroid differentiation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DNF1 encodes a component of a nodule-specific signal peptidase complex required for cleavage/processing of NCR signal peptides in the endoplasmic reticulum. 2. In dnf1 mutants, NCR trafficking to symbiosomes is blocked and bacteroid differentiation does not occur. 3. bacA mutants survive in dnf1 nodules, supporting that BacA is mainly needed against NCR peptide challenge.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Component of nodule-specific signal peptidase complex
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; peptide localization studies; symbiotic phenotyping
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although DNF1 affects peptide trafficking, contribution of other symbiosome-targeted proteins cannot be fully excluded.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss blocks bacteroid differentiation and effective symbiosis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for controlling symbiosome peptide delivery, though likely pleiotropic.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports host nitrogen nutrition via persistent bacteroid function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for persistent, effective bacteroid development; mutation compromises nitrogen fixation and causes abnormal multi-bacteroid symbiosomes and bacteroid lysis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lpsB affects LPS core glycosylation/composition and is important for normal symbiosome organization. 2. lpsB mutants enter host cells but develop multiple bacteroids per symbiosome and fail to persist, with apparent bacteroid lysis. 3. Mutants show increased sensitivity to cationic antimicrobial peptides, linking envelope sugars to protection in symbiosis.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative glycosyl transferase involved in LPS core biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transposon mutant analysis; SDS-PAGE/DOC LPS profiling; symbiotic phenotyping; antimicrobial sensitivity assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article. The exact structural consequence may reflect enrichment of an alternative LPS species rather than simple core truncation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation leads to nitrogen starvation of host and eventual plant death
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Cell-envelope glycan integrity is a relevant target for improving rhizobial persistence in nodules.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to cationic antimicrobial peptides; intracellular symbiosome environment

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stabilizes bacterial outer membrane and supports survival during symbiosis, indirectly enabling nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper VLCFA-modified lipid A biosynthesis; mutants show abnormal bacteroids, stress sensitivity, reduced competitiveness, and impaired symbiotic development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcpXL is the acyl carrier protein on which the lipid A VLCFA is synthesized. 2. acpXL mutants lack VLCFA modification in free-living cells, show increased stress sensitivity, and are less competitive in nodulation. 3. In nodules, acpXL mutants form abnormal/senescing bacteroids and can show partial restoration of VLCFA modification in host-extracted cells.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Very-long-chain fatty acid acyl carrier protein
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lipid A very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rhizobial lipid A VLCFA modification branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries the elongating very-long-chain fatty acid used for lipid A modification rather than catalyzing a chemical conversion itself.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; lipid A structural analysis; competition assays; electron microscopy of bacteroids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Host-extracted acpXL mutant cells can partially restore VLCFA modification, suggesting compensatory ACP usage in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces competitiveness and increases sensitivity to osmotic, pH, and detergent stresses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering robust lipid A modification pathways may improve inoculant competitiveness and nodule persistence.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low pH, osmotic stress, detergents, intracellular symbiosome conditions

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stabilizes bacterial outer membrane and supports symbiotic survival and nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transfers VLCFA to lipid A precursor; mutation causes severe envelope instability, altered acylation, stress hypersensitivity, and abnormal enlarged bacteroids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LpxXL is the acyltransferase that transfers the VLCFA onto lipid A precursor. 2. lpxXL mutants lack the VLCFA modification and leave the VLCFA position unmodified, also generating tri-acylated lipid A species. 3. lpxXL mutants are especially stress-sensitive and form enlarged abnormal bacteroids, indicating a major role of penta-acylated lipid A in membrane stability during symbiosis.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Very-long-chain fatty acid lipid A acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lipid A very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rhizobial lipid A VLCFA modification branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the unusual very-long-chain fatty acid onto the rhizobial lipid A precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell envelope/inner membrane-associated lipid A assembly system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; lipid A profiling; stress assays; bacteroid morphology analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In acpXL mutants, LpxXL may transfer shorter unhydroxylated fatty acids, indicating substrate flexibility.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation strongly increases salt/detergent/pH sensitivity and reduces symbiotic competitiveness
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for strengthening rhizobial envelope robustness in inoculant strains.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low pH, osmotic stress, detergents, symbiosome-associated stress

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: host signaling molecule required for nodulation and infection thread formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rhizobial extracellular signal at root hair interface
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for correct acyl decoration of Nod factor; mutation causes aberrant infection threads and impaired symbiotic establishment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NodF is an ACP required for the specific fatty acyl decoration of Nod factor. 2. nodF mutants lack proper Nod factor acyl decoration. 3. Incorrectly decorated Nod factor leads to aberrant infection threads.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Nod factor acyl carrier protein NodF
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nod factor fatty acyl decoration module
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: acyl-loaded carrier for Nod factor decoration
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries the fatty acyl chain used for specific Nod factor decoration.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasmic fatty acid/Nod factor biosynthesis system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype analysis in infection thread formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in review as a key biosynthetic gene affecting host specificity and infection structure formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation impairs infection thread development and symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning host specificity and nodulation efficiency in engineered rhizobia.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root hair interaction during early symbiosis

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: initiates nodulation signaling required for establishment of nitrogen-fixing symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: rhizobial extracellular signal at root epidermis/root hair interface
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Perceives plant flavonoids and activates nod genes, promoting Nod factor biosynthesis and nodule initiation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NodD recognizes legume-secreted flavonoids. 2. Activated NodD induces expression of bacterial nod genes. 3. This regulation drives Nod factor production essential for nodule development.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates nod gene expression in response to flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of Nod factor signaling molecules
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: early switch activating the nodulation signal biosynthetic program
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of classic genetic and signaling studies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a primary summarized regulatory gene controlling a metabolite central to symbiosis establishment.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defects would block Nod factor production and nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for reprogramming flavonoid responsiveness and host range in rhizobial inoculants.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides fixed nitrogen to the plant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Senses low oxygen and activates nifA expression, enabling nitrogen fixation in bacteroids.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FixL-FixJ constitutes an oxygen-sensing two-component regulatory system. 2. Under microaerobic nodule conditions it regulates expression of nifA. 3. This regulatory cascade is central to activation of nitrogen fixation genes and ammonia production.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FixL sensor kinase; FixJ response regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates nifA expression under low oxygen
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential oxygen-responsive controller of the nitrogen fixation program
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: FixL membrane-associated sensor; FixJ cytoplasmic regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of established genetic/regulatory studies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses this system as part of bacteroid metabolic differentiation rather than as a primary detailed focus.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Improper regulation would impair nitrogenase expression under oxygen-limited conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for engineering rhizobia with improved nitrogen fixation under variable oxygen regimes.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fixed nitrogen source for host growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Master regulator activating nitrogen fixation genes required for ammonia production in bacteroids.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nifA encodes the master regulator of nitrogen fixation. 2. Its expression is controlled by the oxygen-sensing FixJ-FixL system. 3. NifA activation is central to the bacteroid nitrogen fixation program that supplies ammonia to the plant.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: σ54-dependent transcriptional activator-like nitrogen fixation regulator
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator of ammonia-producing nitrogen fixation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary of established nitrogen-fixation genetics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a summarized central regulator of the nutritional output of the symbiosis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key node for optimizing nitrogen fixation efficiency in rhizobial strains.
